integrated_summary.architecture_and_agents: The system is built around a multi-agent architecture.  Key agents include ArchitectAgent, CoderAgent, ContextAgent, DebuggerAgent, GuardianAgent, KnowledgeCuratorAgent, PlannerAgent, PolicyAgent, PostmortemAgent, and TesterAgent.  Each agent is a class inheriting from a BaseAgent, which handles LLM interaction. The ContextAgent plays a central role, managing project context and interacting with human developers when necessary.
integrated_summary.dependencies_and_stack: The project utilizes a range of libraries including Gradio, Pandas, NumPy, OpenAI, Whisper, Sounddevice, Flask, GitPython, SciPy, Scikit-learn, Tree-sitter, and various testing and linting tools.  The tech stack encompasses Python, with a Flask-based API server and Gradio for UI.  Dependencies are managed via `pyproject.toml`.
integrated_summary.knowledge_and_experience: The system leverages LLMs for code generation, debugging, testing, and planning.  The KnowledgeCuratorAgent manages a knowledge base to improve the system's performance over time.  The ContextAgent analyzes the project's codebase and environment to inform agent actions.
integrated_summary.meta_capabilities: The system demonstrates self-healing capabilities through its self-healing cycle and postmortem analysis.  It features code generation, automated testing, debugging, and version control integration.  It also includes audio transcription capabilities using Whisper.
integrated_summary.mission_and_purpose: NexusCore aims to be a self-evolving AI system that assists in software development, automating tasks such as code generation, testing, debugging, and code review.  It strives to improve code quality, increase development speed, and reduce errors.
integrated_summary.policies_and_rules: The PolicyAgent enforces policies and rules to ensure code quality and security.  Policies can be defined and adjusted, allowing for customization to specific project requirements.  The ContextAgent interacts with the human developer to establish and refine these policies.
integrated_summary.testing_philosophy: The project follows a comprehensive testing strategy, including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests.  Test coverage is monitored and enforced.  A significant portion of the codebase is covered by automated tests.
integrated_summary.ui_ux_philosophy: The UI/UX design prioritizes ease of use and intuitive interaction.  The system leverages Gradio for a user-friendly interface, enabling developers to interact with the AI agents seamlessly.  The design aims to minimize friction and allow for efficient collaboration between human developers and AI agents.

integrated_summary.architecture_and_agents: The system is built around a multi-agent architecture.  Key agents include ArchitectAgent, CoderAgent, ContextAgent, DebuggerAgent, GuardianAgent, KnowledgeCuratorAgent, PlannerAgent, PolicyAgent, PostmortemAgent, and TesterAgent.  Each agent is a class inheriting from a BaseAgent class, which handles LLM interaction. The agents collaborate to perform tasks within a defined workflow.
integrated_summary.dependencies_and_stack: The project uses Python 3.10+, along with libraries such as Gradio, Pandas, NumPy, Requests, PyYAML, Python-dotenv, OpenAI, OpenAI-Whisper, Sounddevice, Soundfile, Librosa, PyDub, Google-GenerativeAI, Anthropic, GitPython, SciPy, Scikit-learn, Patch, Unidiff, Tree-sitter, Autopep8, Black, Isort, Flask, Flask-Cors, Werkzeug, and Gunicorn (for production).  Dependencies are managed via pyproject.toml.
integrated_summary.knowledge_and_experience: The system leverages LLMs for code generation, debugging, testing, and other AI tasks.  It incorporates a knowledge base (FKB) to store and retrieve information, and learns from past experiences to improve its performance. The ContextAgent plays a crucial role in managing project context and applying learned knowledge.
integrated_summary.meta_capabilities: The system demonstrates self-healing capabilities through its self-healing cycle.  It can generate tests, debug code, and improve its own performance based on feedback and experience. It integrates voice-to-text functionality for audio input processing and supports multiple LLMs.
integrated_summary.mission_and_purpose: NexusCore aims to automate software development processes, improving efficiency and code quality. It strives to create a self-evolving system that learns from its experiences and adapts to changing requirements.
integrated_summary.policies_and_rules: The system incorporates policies and rules to guide its actions and prevent errors.  These policies are defined and managed through the PolicyAgent and can be customized through a Gradio interface. The ContextAgent interacts with the human developer to establish policies.
integrated_summary.testing_philosophy: The testing philosophy emphasizes comprehensive testing, including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests.  The TesterAgent generates tests, and the system uses pytest for test execution.  Code quality is also assessed using tools like pylint, mypy, and bandit.
integrated_summary.ui_ux_philosophy: The UI/UX is designed for ease of use and interaction.  It utilizes Gradio for a user-friendly interface, allowing developers to interact with the system through a web-based application.  The UI includes features for code input, output display, test execution, and feedback provision.

integrated_summary.architecture_and_agents: The system is built around a multi-agent architecture.  Key agents include ArchitectAgent, CoderAgent, ContextAgent, DebuggerAgent, GuardianAgent, KnowledgeCuratorAgent, PlannerAgent, PolicyAgent, PostmortemAgent, and RequirementAgent. Each agent is a class inheriting from a BaseAgent class, which handles LLM interaction.  Agents utilize the LLMRouter to select appropriate LLMs (Gemini, OpenAI, Local).
integrated_summary.dependencies_and_stack: The project uses Python 3.10+, Flask, Gradio, OpenAI, Whisper, and various other libraries for audio processing, code analysis (pylint, mypy, bandit, pytest-cov), Git interaction, and more.  Dependencies are managed via `pyproject.toml`.
integrated_summary.knowledge_and_experience: The system leverages a knowledge base (FKB) which is updated and validated by the KnowledgeCuratorAgent.  The DebuggerAgent uses this knowledge base to assist in debugging. ContextAgent loads and manages project context, including tech stack and dependencies.
integrated_summary.meta_capabilities: The system demonstrates self-healing capabilities through its agents. It can generate code, tests, and commit messages.  It incorporates audio transcription, code analysis, debugging, and version control.  It has a Next-Price-Engine (NPE) to manage costs of LLM calls.
integrated_summary.mission_and_purpose: NexusCore aims to automate software development, providing a self-evolving system that can design, code, test, debug, and improve software projects. It uses a multi-agent approach to handle different aspects of the development lifecycle.
integrated_summary.policies_and_rules: The PolicyAgent enforces policies and rules to ensure code quality and security. Policies can be defined and customized. The ContextAgent interacts with a policy interface (potentially Gradio-based) to gather human input on development policies.
integrated_summary.testing_philosophy: The system uses a comprehensive testing strategy, including unit tests and integration tests.  Testing is automated and integrated into the development cycle.  Test generation is handled by TesterAgent.  Extensive unit tests are present in the `tests` directory.
integrated_summary.ui_ux_philosophy: The UI/UX is designed around Gradio, providing an interactive interface for users to interact with the system.  Streamlit elements are also incorporated.  The UI allows for code generation, testing, debugging, and project management.

integrated_summary.mission_and_purpose: NexusCoreプロジェクトのコード資産を走査し、構造と変更を記録する。
integrated_summary.architecture_and_agents: ディレクトリ単位の構成/エージェント群は AST 概略から推測可。詳細は各モジュールのクラス/関数一覧参照。
integrated_summary.dependencies_and_stack: imports 集計から主要依存を推測。
integrated_summary.testing_philosophy: tests ディレクトリの存在/規模から基礎的なテスト方針を推測。
integrated_summary.ui_ux_philosophy: app/tools 等のUI関連コード有無を俯瞰。
integrated_summary.policies_and_rules: config/policy等の存在で運用ルールの痕跡を確認。
integrated_summary.knowledge_and_experience: evaluation/exports など運用上の知見資産を確認可能。
integrated_summary.meta_capabilities: 差分検出・イベント記録・スナップショット可視化に対応。
